item.title: Replimune RP1 Melanoma Drug FDA Rejection
item.description:
• Summary: Replimune's oncolytic virus therapy RP1 for advanced melanoma has been rejected by the FDA for a second time, based on unresolved concerns about clinical trial data. The agency cited issues with the CERPASS trial, which evaluated RP1 in combination with the PD-1 inhibitor cemiplimab versus cemiplimab alone, questioning the trial design and efficacy endpoints. This complete response letter delays approval in the competitive melanoma treatment landscape, impacting Replimune's development timeline.
  • MoAs: RP1: Genetically engineered herpes simplex virus type 1 that selectively replicates in and lyses tumor cells, while stimulating anti-tumor immune responses.
  • Sources: Biospace, Biopharmadive, Endpoints News, StatNews

item.title: OriCell CAR-T Therapy & Financing
item.description:
• Summary: OriCell, a China-based biotech, has raised $40 million in additional financing as it prepares for an initial public offering. The company is developing CAR-T therapies targeting glypican-3 (GPC3) for hepatocellular carcinoma (liver cancer), with the funds supporting clinical trials and expansion. This highlights growing interest in CAR-T for solid tumors and China's role in the global biopharma market.
  • MoAs: OriCell CAR-T therapy: Chimeric antigen receptor T-cells engineered to target GPC3 on liver cancer cells, leading to targeted cell killing.
  • Sources: Biopharmadive, Endpoints News, Fierce Biotech
